reviewer.evidence: Applies to candidate.c (the w/h named-constants form, floor 2): PASS all 6 tests (semantic content: rect width/height stored every iteration; natural C idiom for loop constants; SOTN-accepted named-intermediate declaration order family). THREE sandbox-0 closing forms for the residual lh/sh cluster have now FAILED review and are preserved under rejected/ — they are NOT usable: id-ptr-alias (load-side /s flip), byte-offset-cast (load-side /s flip), array-retype-store (store-side /s flip, session 2).

remaining_gap.summary: 3-position cluster at maspsx idx 21-23: cc1 sched1 hoists the post-store reload `lh v0,8(a0)` above `sh a1,D_800A9A20`; target has store first. Mechanism fully characterized AND the closing-form space is now exhausted on both axes: the only sched1 edge that keeps target's order is the store->load true_dependence, which the escape clause (sched.c ~816) drops whenever (load /s=1 + varying) vs (store /s=0 + fixed). Flipping the LOAD to /s=0 (pointer-var deref, byte-offset cast) and flipping the STORE to /s=1 (s16[2] array re-type) all reach sandbox 0 with identical bytes and ALL THREE FAILED adversarial cheat review as same-bytes alias-analysis coercion. No non-/s-flag lever exists: the lh's chain priority (depth ~5 vs sh's 2) always wins absent the dependence edge, stores have no downstream chain to extend, both insns share one basic block, and neither mem is QImode.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD carries `reorder 22,21 @ 21-22` (lh/sh swap, the open cluster) and `reorder 44,45,43 @ 43-45` (preheader, retired by the candidate's w/h lever). Committing the candidate alone would require rewriting the preheader rule for the shifted order — not a sanctioned end state; close both clusters or stay at HEAD.

next_hypotheses: USER POLICY DECISION (now the SOLE remaining avenue; the 2026-06-10 session-2 reviewer's own next_action endorses surfacing it as NEEDS_USER): the emitted order (store-then-reload, one basic block, identical insn multiset) is only producible by GCC 2.7.2 when the store/load pair's MEM_IN_STRUCT_P flags differ from what the natural scalar-global + pointer-index spellings produce (verified against expr.c:4568-4577 + sched.c:816-841 + RTL dumps). That is mechanical evidence the original 1998 source spelled one side differently — a /s=0 read spelling (pointer deref / cast) or an aggregate-typed store target. The reviewer holds this proves 'SOME such spelling', not a specific one, and that any same-bytes respelling chosen for its /s effect is the forbidden coercion family. The user judgment call: does compiler-mechanism evidence of the original spelling CLASS sanction committing one representative spelling (and if so which: the byte-offset cast, the pointer local, or the s16[2] array)? Until answered, the function stays at HEAD (2 rules) with this candidate at floor 2.
